title: TUI run() event loop has no shutdown signal to stop in-flight API tasks
description:
## Severity
Major
## Dimension
concurrency
## Location
src/tui/app.rs:526-589
## Violation
The run() event loop terminates when should_quit is true, but spawn_api_turn pushes background tasks that hold senders into the same channel. In-flight requests keep running after the user quits — tools mid-execution have no way to know.

The agentic follow-up loop in run_api_turn_async (line 1518) has no cancellation token: a 25-iteration agentic chain continues billing the user's API account after quit.

## Mandated refactor
Add tokio_util::sync::CancellationToken on App, cloned into ApiTurnParams. run_api_turn_async checks token.is_cancelled() at each iteration. pipeline::run_turn races against token.cancelled(). On should_quit, cancel token before breaking the loop.

## Filed from QA review dispatch
